events.note: Starting scope — pushing intent check for coverage expansion and data gap fixes

events.change_summary: Initial intent check — expand coverage to top 50 countries, address data gaps

events.summary: Intent check pushed — expands coverage to top 50 countries and addresses output data gaps. Flags ambiguity about which specific data gaps to tackle.

events.note: Writing full scope (revision 2) for coverage top-50 + output data gap fills

events.change_summary: Full scope: expand coverage to top 50 countries; fill identified output data gaps (Radar overlay for uncovered/remote-only ISPs; resilient partial baseline speedtest)

events.rationale: Primarily fills incomplete/broken output behavior (blank ISP rows, discarded partial baseline) plus a one-line coverage default change. No new web UI — all output is existing Rich terminal patterns, so skip design. Well-contained to cli.py/display.py/speedtest.py building on existing patterns (reuses _fetch_rum, probe_errors convention), no new subsystems or system boundaries, so skip architecture. Detailed phases with Files/Verification written in-scope.

events.decision: Interpret "cover all the gaps" as the two concrete, observable gaps in the pasted country US run — (1) blank "no coverage"/"remote-only" ISP rows that never fetch ASN-level Cloudflare Radar data because they short-circuit before _measure(), and (2) speedtest.run() discarding a successful direction when the other fails. Backfill Radar via the existing _fetch_rum path rather than routing skipped ASNs through _measure() (which would trigger unwanted trace probes toward dead targets). Reverse-DNS hop enrichment and per-ISP throughput are explicitly deferred and flagged as an open question for the builder.
events.rationale: "Cover all the gaps" is open-ended; committing to the observable gaps keeps the task bounded and verifiable while surfacing the ambiguity to the builder instead of silently guessing scope. Reusing _fetch_rum avoids side effects and matches existing patterns.

events.summary: Full scope pushed as revision 2. Scope = coverage top-50 (one-line default change) + closing two concrete output gaps from the pasted country US run: (1) backfill Cloudflare Radar quality data for "no coverage"/"remote-only" ISP rows that today skip _measure() and render blank, surfacing it in both the per-ISP section and summary; (2) make speedtest.run() return partial results so an upload timeout no longer discards a successful download. 6 requirements, 7 acceptance criteria, 2 phases. Workflow: bug-fix / standard (scope → implementation → review → release; design and architecture skipped). Because "cover all the gaps" is open-ended, I flagged the open question in Technical Notes — reverse-DNS hop enrichment and per-ISP throughput are deferred/out of scope pending your confirmation.

events.note: Adding Phase 3 (PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery) as revision 3

events.change_summary: Added Phase 3: PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery to fill the "no trace target" gap at its source. ASN-pair path analysis split into a separate proposed task (not in this scope).

events.summary: Revision 3 pushed. Added Phase 3 — PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery — reusing the PeeringDB client already in ixp.py to extend country.get_test_ip_for_asn with a non-RIPE source of reachable per-ASN targets (the ASN's IXP interface IPs). This fills the "no trace target" gap at its source; Radar backfill (Phase 2) remains the fallback for ASNs still dark. Scope is now 8 requirements, 9 acceptance criteria, 3 phases; workflow unchanged (bug-fix/standard, no design/architecture). I left existing RIPE Atlas target lookup in place and flagged the "remove RIPE entirely?" question in Technical Notes since ripping it out affects all of country mode. Separately, the ASN-to-ASN path analysis feature (hybrid BGP-via-RouteViews/bgp.tools + opportunistic live Globalping traceroute) is proposed as its own new task — it depends on this task's PeeringDB discovery.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: coverage default raised to 50

events.summary: Raised coverage command --top default from 20 to 50. Title already interpolates top so no title change needed. All 146 tests pass, ruff clean.

items.description: Run `netpath coverage` with no flag and confirm the table title reads `Globalping Coverage — Top 50 Countries` and lists up to 50 ranked country rows.
Run `netpath coverage --top 10` and confirm exactly 10 rows appear with a `Top 10 Countries` title (override still works).
Run `netpath coverage -t 5` and confirm the short flag also overrides to 5 rows / `Top 5 Countries`.

events.note: Phase 2: backfill Radar for uncovered/remote-only ISP rows + resilient partial baseline speedtest

events.description: Starting phase 2: fill uncovered/remote-only rows + salvage partial baselines

events.details: 146 baseline + 4 new tests/test_speedtest.py partial-baseline tests. ruff check src tests clean.

alternatives_considered: Keep run() raising and only salvage partial results in the baseline caller — rejected because it leaves the _measure() fallback still discarding a good direction and duplicates try/except logic.
events.decision: Implementation: speedtest.run() now never raises on a single-direction failure — it returns {download, upload, server, errors} with per-direction results (None on failure). extract_stats() returns (upload|None, download|None). Both the baseline caller and the _measure() speedtest fallback were updated to consume partial results, so a successful download survives an upload timeout in every path (not just the baseline).
events.rationale: Mirrors the existing probe_errors convention (per-probe error strings, best-effort partial results). Changing run() at the source rather than only patching the baseline caller means the _measure() fallback also stops discarding a good direction — a strict improvement consistent with R7 with no extra risk since _measure already tolerated a fully-failed speedtest.

events.change_summary: Phase 2 complete: Radar backfill for uncovered/remote-only rows + resilient partial baseline

events.summary: Backfilled Cloudflare Radar for remote-only/no-coverage ISP rows (per-ISP panel + summary-tree figures via _fetch_rum, no live target), and made speedtest.run() return partial results so a failed direction no longer discards the one that succeeded. Baseline panel now shows the succeeded direction and marks the failed one; both-fail preserves the warning. 150 tests pass, ruff clean.

items.description: Run `NETPATH_CF_TOKEN=<radar:read token> netpath country US` and confirm at least one formerly `remote-only` or `no coverage` ISP now prints a `Cloudflare Radar` panel in its section, and shows a `Radar: ↓ … ↑ … idle …` line under that ISP in the `US summary` tree.
Run `netpath country US` with NO `NETPATH_CF_TOKEN` set and confirm remote-only / no-coverage rows render exactly as before — bare labels, no empty Radar values or panels.

Simulate a partial baseline: on a link where the upload times out (or throttle upload), run `netpath country US` and confirm the `Your baseline · speed.cloudflare.com` panel still shows the `↓ Download` figure and marks `↑ Upload` as `failed`, instead of only printing a warning.

Confirm the both-directions-fail case still behaves as before: with the network fully blocked to `speed.cloudflare.com`, `netpath country US` prints the `Baseline speedtest failed:` warning and no baseline panel.
Run `uv run pytest -q` and confirm 150 passed, then `uv run ruff check src tests` reports `All checks passed!`.

events.note: Phase 3: PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery in ixp.py + country.get_test_ip_for_asn

events.description: Implementing PeeringDB netixlan lookup in ixp.py and target discovery in country.py

events.details: 150 baseline + 3 new tests/test_country.py PeeringDB netixlan tests (fallback returns netixlan IPv4, absent ASN unchanged, per-ASN caching). Existing Atlas-empty/error tests updated to also stub the PeeringDB layer. ruff check src tests clean.

events.decision: Implementation: added get_test_target_for_asn(asn) -> (ipv4, origin) as the origin-aware primitive; get_test_ip_for_asn now wraps it and returns just the IPv4 (contract preserved). Atlas probe is tried first, then PeeringDB netixlan IPv4 as a second non-RIPE source. PeeringDB netixlan lookup (netixlan_ipv4_for_asn + _load_netixlan with a per-ASN in-process cache) lives in ixp.py, reusing the existing PeeringDB access pattern.
events.rationale: The acceptance criteria pin get_test_ip_for_asn to a plain IPv4 return, but the CLI note needs to know the target's origin to label it correctly. Splitting into a tuple-returning primitive keeps the tested string contract intact while giving the CLI the origin without a second lookup. Putting netixlan in ixp.py follows the scope's directive to reuse the existing PeeringDB client/cache rather than add a second one. Selection is deterministic (first record with a usable ipaddr4).

events.change_summary: Phase 3 complete: PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery

events.summary: Added PeeringDB netixlan as a second, non-RIPE trace-target source. ixp.py gained netixlan_ipv4_for_asn (+ _load_netixlan with a per-ASN in-process cache) reusing the existing PeeringDB access pattern. country.get_test_target_for_asn tries Atlas first, then PeeringDB netixlan IPv4; get_test_ip_for_asn wraps it with its string contract unchanged. CLI trace note now labels the target origin (PeeringDB IXP vs Atlas probe). 153 tests pass, ruff clean.

items.description: Run `netpath country US` and look for an ISP that previously showed `no coverage` or `remote-only`. If it peers at an IXP in PeeringDB, confirm it now prints a trace section with a note reading `(PeeringDB IXP trace target — no iperf3 server in ASxxxx)`.
Confirm an ISP with a connected RIPE Atlas probe still shows the `(Atlas probe trace target — …)` note (Atlas is preferred over PeeringDB).

Spot-check a PeeringDB lookup directly: `python -c "from netpath.ixp import netixlan_ipv4_for_asn; print(netixlan_ipv4_for_asn('AS3356'))"` and confirm it prints a real IXP interface IPv4 (or `None` if AS3356 has no netixlan IPv4).
Confirm an enterprise/DoD ASN with no Atlas probe and no PeeringDB presence (e.g. `AS56`) still renders as `no coverage` — unchanged behavior.
Run `uv run pytest -q` and confirm `153 passed`, then `uv run ruff check src tests` reports `All checks passed!`.

events.summary: All phases approved — implementation ready for review.

Both pytest (153/153) and ruff pass.

All three phases verified: coverage default at cli.py:980 is 50; PeeringDB netixlan fallback in ixp.py/country.py is correctly wired and unit-tested; Radar backfill in both short-circuit branches correctly gates on cf_token and rum presence; resilient speedtest is independently unit-tested for all four cases.

events.summary: APPROVE — all 9 ACs pass, pytest 153/153, ruff clean. Coverage default is 50, PeeringDB netixlan fallback is wired and tested, Radar backfill for uncovered rows correctly gates on token presence, and the resilient partial speedtest is unit-tested for all four cases.

workflow.rationale: Primarily fills incomplete/broken output behavior (blank ISP rows, discarded partial baseline) plus a one-line coverage default change. No new web UI — all output is existing Rich terminal patterns, so skip design. Well-contained to cli.py/display.py/speedtest.py building on existing patterns (reuses _fetch_rum, probe_errors convention), no new subsystems or system boundaries, so skip architecture. stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Expand coverage to top 50

Raised the `coverage` command's `--top` default from 20 to 50 (`src/netpath/cli.py`). The table title already interpolates `top`, so no title change was needed.

**Files changed:** `src/netpath/cli.py` (one line).

## Phase 2: Fill uncovered/remote-only ISP data and salvage partial baselines

Closed two concrete output gaps from the pasted `country US` run.

**Resilient baseline speedtest (R7/R8):**
- `speedtest.run()` no longer raises when one direction fails. It attempts download and upload independently and returns `{"download": {...}|None, "upload": {...}|None, "server": ..., "errors": {direction: reason}}`, mirroring the `probe_errors` convention.
- `speedtest.extract_stats()` returns `(upload|None, download|None)` so callers render whatever succeeded.
- Both consumers updated: the baseline block (`cli.py`) and the `_measure()` speedtest fallback (`cli.py`). A successful download now survives an upload timeout in every path. Both-directions-fail preserves the original warning with no panel.
- `display.baseline_panel(upload, download, errors=...)` now tolerates a None direction and marks the failed one as `failed` rather than dropping the whole panel.

**Radar backfill for blank rows (R5/R6):**
- The two short-circuit branches (`remote_only`, `skip_reason`/no-coverage) now call the existing `_fetch_rum(asn_str, cf_token)` before `continue`. When Radar data returns, `display.rum_only_panel` prints it in the per-ISP section and the Radar dict is stored on the summary row under a `rum` key (matching what `_measure` sets).
- `display.country_summary` renders a compact Radar figures subrow (`_render_rum_subrow` / `_rum_summary_str`) beneath remote-only and no-coverage rows when `rum` is present. With no token, `_fetch_rum` returns None and rows render exactly as before — no empty values.

**Tests:** `tests/test_speedtest.py` — upload-fails/download-succeeds returns partial (no raise), download-fails/upload-succeeds, both-fail records both errors, both-succeed.

**Files changed:** `src/netpath/speedtest.py`, `src/netpath/cli.py`, `src/netpath/display.py`, `tests/test_speedtest.py`.

## Phase 3: PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery

Added a second, non-RIPE source of reachable trace targets (R2-R4).

**PeeringDB netixlan lookup (`src/netpath/ixp.py`):**
- `_load_netixlan(asn)` fetches `https://www.peeringdb.com/api/netixlan?asn=N` (bare integer via `normalize_asn(asn)[2:]`) and caches the record list per-ASN in a module-level `_NETIXLAN_CACHE` dict, reusing the same PeeringDB access/caching pattern as the IXP prefix classifier already in this module. Any failure yields an empty list.
- `netixlan_ipv4_for_asn(asn)` returns the first record with a usable `ipaddr4` (deterministic selection), or None.

**Target discovery (`src/netpath/country.py`):**
- New `get_test_target_for_asn(asn) -> (ipv4, origin)` tries a connected RIPE Atlas probe first (origin `"atlas"`), then falls back to a PeeringDB netixlan IPv4 (origin `"peeringdb"`), else `(None, None)`. Prefix-guessing remains removed.
- `get_test_ip_for_asn(asn)` now wraps `get_test_target_for_asn` and returns just the IPv4 — its plain-string contract is unchanged, so existing behavior and the acceptance-criteria tests hold.

**Origin note (`src/netpath/cli.py`):**
- The per-ISP trace target note now reads `PeeringDB IXP trace target` vs `Atlas probe trace target` based on the origin returned by `get_test_target_for_asn`, so the user can see where the target came from.

**Tests (`tests/test_country.py`):**
- New: PeeringDB fallback returns the netixlan IPv4 with origin `"peeringdb"`; ASN absent from netixlan returns `(None, None)` (no regression); per-ASN caching (second lookup served from cache, one HTTP call).
- Updated the existing Atlas-empty / Atlas-error / null-address tests to also stub the PeeringDB layer so they stay offline and assert the no-target path.

**Files changed:** `src/netpath/ixp.py`, `src/netpath/country.py`, `src/netpath/cli.py`, `tests/test_country.py`.

**Note:** existing RIPE Atlas target lookup was left in place (Atlas tried first, PeeringDB as fallback) per the flagged open question in the scope — removing RIPE entirely was not confirmed.

**Verification:** 153 tests pass (150 + 3 new); `ruff check src tests` clean. Live PeeringDB reachability and the in-`country`-mode origin note are covered in the manual test list.
stageContent.review:
## Verdict

**Verdict:** APPROVE
**Files reviewed:** 6 files changed across 3 phases

All 9 acceptance criteria pass. Coverage default is 50, PeeringDB netixlan fallback is correctly wired, Radar backfill renders for uncovered rows when a token is present and is suppressed without one, and partial speedtest behavior is unit-tested. Both pytest (153/153) and ruff pass.

### Automated Checks

| Check | Result | Details |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Test suite (pytest) | PASS | 153 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped |
| Lint (ruff) | PASS | All checks passed |

### Acceptance Criteria (9/9 passed)

- [x] AC-1: Running `netpath coverage` with no `--top` flag prints up to 50 ranked country rows and a title reading "Top 50 Countries"; verified against the default at `cli.py:960`. — PASS: cli.py:980 — `top: int = typer.Option(50, "--top", "-t", ...)`. Title at cli.py:997: `f"[bold]Globalping Coverage — Top {top} Countries[/bold]"`. Default produces 50-row table titled "Top 50 Countries".
- [x] AC-2: Running `netpath coverage --top 10` still prints exactly 10 rows with a "Top 10" title (override preserved). — PASS: cli.py:992 — `ranked = sorted(...)[:top]` slices to `top`. Title interpolates `top`, so `--top 10` yields `[:10]` and "Top 10 Countries".
- [x] AC-3: For an ASN with IXP presence in PeeringDB but no other live target, `get_test_ip_for_asn` returns a PeeringDB `netixlan` IPv4; verified by a unit test mocking the PeeringDB response. — PASS: tests/test_country.py:70-87 — `test_get_test_ip_falls_back_to_peeringdb_netixlan` mocks Atlas to empty and `ixp.requests.get` to return `{"data": [{"ipaddr4": null}, {"ipaddr4": "80.249.208.100", ...}]}`. Asserts ip=="80.249.208.100" and origin=="peeringdb".
- [x] AC-4: For an ASN absent from PeeringDB `netixlan`, `get_test_ip_for_asn` behaves exactly as today (no regression); verified by a unit test. — PASS: tests/test_country.py:90-100 — `test_get_test_ip_no_peeringdb_presence_unchanged` mocks both Atlas and PeeringDB to return empty. Asserts `(ip, origin) == (None, None)`.
- [x] AC-5: In `country` mode, an ISP that gains a PeeringDB target shows a trace section with a note indicating the target's origin. — PASS: cli.py:756-762 — `origin_label = "PeeringDB IXP trace target" if target_origin == "peeringdb" else "Atlas probe trace target"`. Printed as `f"→ {test_ip}  ({origin_label} — no iperf3 server in {asn_str})"`.
- [x] AC-6: In `country` mode with a Cloudflare token, an ISP that is still remote-only / no-coverage but exists in Radar shows a Radar panel in its section and Radar figures in the summary tree. — PASS: cli.py:786-794 and 802-810 — both branches call `_fetch_rum(asn_str, cf_token)` and `display.rum_only_panel` when non-None, storing `"rum": _rum` on the summary row. display.py:567 and 579 call `_render_rum_subrow` for these rows.
- [x] AC-7: In `country` mode with no Cloudflare token, remote-only and no-coverage rows render exactly as today (no empty Radar values). — PASS: With no token, `_fetch_rum` returns None. `if _rum:` at cli.py:787/803 is False — no panel printed. `_render_rum_subrow` at display.py:448-450: `if not rum: return` — nothing rendered.
- [x] AC-8: With `speedtest._upload` mocked to raise and `_download` mocked to succeed, `run()` returns the download plus a recorded upload error instead of raising; verified by a unit test. Both-fail case preserves the warning with no panel. — PASS: speedtest.py:94-102 — independent try/except per direction; failures set `result["errors"][direction]`. tests/test_speedtest.py:11-26 asserts upload-fails/download-succeeds returns partial (no raise). Lines 44-57 assert both-fail records both errors.
- [x] AC-9: `pytest` and `ruff check src tests` both pass. — PASS: pytest: 153 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped. ruff check src tests: All checks passed.

### Code Quality (Refactor Review)

No code quality issues found in changed files.

### Security Assessment (Security Review)

No security issues found in changed files.

### Decisions Made During Implementation

- Coverage default raised from 20 to 50 via a one-line `typer.Option` default change; title already interpolated `top` so no further changes needed.
- speedtest.run() redesigned to attempt each direction independently and return {download, upload, server, errors}, mirroring the probe_errors convention; both callers updated.
- Radar backfill for remote-only/no-coverage rows calls the existing _fetch_rum() directly rather than routing through _measure(), avoiding unwanted trace probes toward dead targets.
- PeeringDB netixlan lookup added to ixp.py reusing existing PeeringDB HTTP client + module-level cache pattern; get_test_target_for_asn added as origin-aware primitive; get_test_ip_for_asn wraps it to preserve the plain-string contract.
- Existing RIPE Atlas probe lookup preserved as primary target source; PeeringDB netixlan is fallback only — removing Atlas entirely was not confirmed.

## Required Changes

None.

decisions: Coverage default raised from 20 to 50 via a one-line `typer.Option` default change; title already interpolated `top` so no further changes needed.
speedtest.run() redesigned to attempt each direction independently and return {download, upload, server, errors}, mirroring the probe_errors convention; both callers updated.
Radar backfill for remote-only/no-coverage rows calls the existing _fetch_rum() directly rather than routing through _measure(), avoiding unwanted trace probes toward dead targets.

PeeringDB netixlan lookup added to ixp.py reusing existing PeeringDB HTTP client + module-level cache pattern; get_test_target_for_asn added as origin-aware primitive; get_test_ip_for_asn wraps it to preserve the plain-string contract.
Existing RIPE Atlas probe lookup preserved as primary target source; PeeringDB netixlan is fallback only — removing Atlas entirely was not confirmed.

changeAnnotations.note: Raises coverage default from 20 to 50; adds Radar backfill calls in remote-only/no-coverage branches; updates baseline handler for partial results; switches target-note label to reflect PeeringDB vs Atlas origin.

Redesigns run() to attempt each direction independently and return partial results with per-direction errors instead of raising on a single failure; extract_stats() tolerates None in either direction.
Adds baseline_panel errors parameter to mark failed directions; adds _rum_summary_str/_render_rum_subrow helpers; country_summary now calls _render_rum_subrow for remote-only and no-coverage rows.
Adds _load_netixlan and netixlan_ipv4_for_asn to expose PeeringDB IXP interface IPs per-ASN, reusing the existing PeeringDB HTTP client and module-level caching pattern.
Adds get_test_target_for_asn returning (ipv4, origin); get_test_ip_for_asn now wraps it; PeeringDB netixlan used as fallback when no Atlas probe exists.
New test file covering the four speedtest partial-result cases: upload-fails, download-fails, both-fail, both-succeed.
Three new PeeringDB tests added; existing Atlas-empty/error/null-address tests updated to also stub the PeeringDB layer for offline isolation.
